protocols.notes_updates: IMMEDIATE MANAGEMENT: ABCD approach to initial resuscitation | Don't ever forget glucose | Hypoglycaemia: 1ml/kg of 50% Dextrose IV | Airway and Breathing: Patient position - lateral | Supplemental oxygen (nasal prongs/FMO2) | Rapid Sequence Intubation (RSI) if seizures continue for > 5 minutes (see RSI protocol) | See Status Epilepticus protocol for further care | Circulation: Insert an IV line | Shock: Fluid bolus 20ml/kg of Balsol/Ringers Lactate | Drugs: Glucose if hypoglycaemia | Benzodiazepine to abort seizure | HISTORY: Detailed history of seizure | NB! Recent history of head trauma? | Drug use/abuse/overdose? | PHYSICAL EXAMINATION: Vital signs | General examination and any evidence of underlying diseases, including trauma | Neurological examination | Signs of meningitis. NB Crypto can be subtle | Focal neurological deficit | INVESTIGATIONS: ECG and blood glucose on ALL patients | Tailor further investigations to clinical findings | Urgent CT brain with contrast if: No return to baseline | Focal neurological deficit | Features of raised ICP | DISCHARGE: Return to baseline | No significant concerns on clinical examination | Note: DO NOT require anti-epileptic treatment | Follow up at Neuro OPD for workup | ADMISSION CRITERIA: Comorbidities e.g. renal failure, malnutrition | Age > 60 years | Immunocompromise | Known cardiovascular disease | Pregnancy | Focal neurology/delirium | Toxins | Trauma | Malignancy

drugs.notes: For hypoglycaemia. Don't ever forget glucose check
To abort seizure. Specific agent and dose per local protocol (see Status Epilepticus protocol)
